rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE ET AL VS. SUTTER HEALTH, A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 16, 2024, line 1, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE, JOHN DOE MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing required. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Documents Related To Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication And Notice Of Lodging Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 2.551b3aiii
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 16, 2024, line 2, 2 - PLAINTIFFS ERIN BRINKMAN, LIEFF, CABRASER, HEIMANN, & BERNSTEIN, LLP, ANNE SHAVER'S Motion For Leave To File Documents Related To Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication And Notice Of Lodging Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 2.551b3aiii Jane Doe's unopposed sealing motion is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and complies with the findings requirements of CRC 2.550 and specifically lists the sealed information and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ERIN BRINKMAN ESQ ET AL VS. JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 16, 2024, line 2, 1 - PLAINTIFFS ERIN BRINKMAN, LIEFF, CABRASER, HEIMANN, & BERNSTEIN, LLP, ANNE SHAVER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Cross-defendants' motion for summary judgment is granted as to the remaining causes of action in the cross-complaint. In its order of November 12, 2024, the court concluded that the parties negotiated a separate fee and the 35% contingency fee did not apply. The record in this motion confirms that conclusion. Cross-complainant Doe's claims, which are predicated on the enforceability of the contingency fee agreement, therefore fail. The evidence shows that attorney Brinkman discussed Doe's recovery from Bank One with Doe, Doe received a copy of the MOU, Doe signed the settlement agreement and Doe cashed her settlement check. (UMF 30, 33, 37, 39.) The evidence also shows that Doe was aware of the Bank Two settlement, the funds she was to receive and she consented to the settlement. (Cross-Complaint, par. 16; UMF 47-49.) The above undisputed facts are dispositive and cross-defendants are entitled to summary judgment on the cross-complaint. The court overrules Doe's parol evidence objections. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance With A Court Order, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Monday, December 16, 2024, Line 2. PLAINTIFF MICKEY QIU's Of Motion To Compel Compliance With A Court Order, And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiffs motion to compel compliance with a court order, and request for sanctions is denied. Defendants say plaintiff did not engage in the required meet-and-confer to resolve the discovery dispute (Opp. 3:3-13; CCP 2016.040)  a fact the reply brief does not dispute. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: ALBERT NORRIS MCCRUTCHEON III VS. CROSS MEDIA INTERNATIONAL, INC., A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Mccrutcheon And Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Same
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Monday, December 16, 2024, Line 3. DEFENDANT CROSS MEDIA INTERNATIONAL, INC.'s Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Mccrutcheon And Production Of Documents; Request For Sanctions. Defendants motion to compel plaintiffs deposition in this slip-and-fall case is granted; the deposition shall be taken on or before January 30, 2025. Plaintiff does not dispute that he must sit for the deposition, but scheduling it has proved acrimonious. Plaintiff and his counsel shall pay a $1,000 sanction in the hope (likely in vain) that all counsel will behave like adults in the future. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: JOHN CRESS VS. SPEAR INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Cross-Defendant'S Special Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 16, 2024, line 4, DEFENDANT MIGUEL YNCLINO'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Cross-Defendant'S Special Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint; "Defendant's motion for reconsideration of order granting cross-defendant's special motion to strike cross-complaint" [anti-SLAPP] is denied. A reconsideration motion must be "based on new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) Defendant says a "March 10, 2023 email represents new evidence." (11/22/24 Memo.) But that email is 20 months old and defendant discussed it in his 10/29/24 anti-SLAPP memorandum. (See, e.g., id. at 2:4-7.) Defendant also argues that the court's anti-SLAPP order "creates new legal circumstances." (11/22/24 Memo. 4:7-11.) But that would be so in virtually every case where a court order is filed, eviscerating CCP 1008. None of the legal authority relied on by defendant's 11/22/24 memo is new. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/RBU)
